question: Who holds the money-transmitter / MSB license when a wallet uses a Stellar anchor to on/off-ramp fiat — the wallet, the anchor, or Stellar?

golden.answer:
Do not decide licensing from the word anchor. Identify each participating legal entity and ask who accepts, transmits, exchanges, issues, redeems, or custodies fiat/crypto for another person; who controls hosted-wallet value; which customer/transaction jurisdictions apply; and whether an exemption, agent, bank, payment partner, or other structure changes the perimeter.

An on/off-ramp operator doing fiat/crypto exchange or transmission is commonly regulated, but the exact US analysis separates **FinCEN MSB registration** from **state money-transmitter licensing** and remains facts-and-circumstances. A custodial wallet or program operator can also be in scope; noncustodial signing software alone generally is not. Each route participant assesses its own duties.

Stellar/SDF is not automatically the licensee merely because the network settles a transfer, but that statement is bounded by the actual network/SDF activity and custody posture. This is a classification framework, not legal advice; use current primary law and counsel for each jurisdiction.
keyFacts: Allocates obligations by legal entity, activity, custody/control, route, and jurisdiction—not the anchor label.
Separates federal MSB registration from state money-transmitter licensing.
Explains that wallets/operators may be covered based on custody/activity while infrastructure alone is not automatically the licensee.
avoid: Do NOT say every anchor holds one universal MSB/money-transmitter license.
Do NOT call FinCEN registration a license or ignore state/jurisdictional rules.
Do NOT give definitive legal advice from an Anchor Directory label.
golden.notes:
Anchor responsibility model is first-party (anchors are regulated financial institutions; the protocol/SDF is not a licensee). Confidence raised draft->high on the durable framing; specific jurisdictional requirements are not asserted. Trap: licensing on the protocol.
Also good if the answer: The wallet/app may also have its own MSB/money-transmitter obligations depending on custody and jurisdiction. | Anchors must map licenses to every jurisdiction in which they onboard customers.
Golden cites: developers.stellar.org anchor docs; reputable regulatory reference where relevant.
CORRECTION 2026-07-10: reject noun-based legal allocation. Require entity/activity/custody/route/jurisdiction analysis and distinguish registration from licensing.

corroboration.claim: The anchor always holds the MSB/money-transmitter license for the fiat leg

evidence.ref: A: eCFR/FinCEN acceptance/transmission and hosted/unhosted guidance
evidence.note: verbatim legacy evidence descriptor imported at migration

evidence.ref: A: network/software exclusions and federal registration rules

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The case categorically assigned an MSB/money-transmitter license to the anchor. Duties attach to actual legal entities and regulated activities; federal MSB registration and state money-transmitter licensing are distinct, and custodial wallets or other route participants may also be in scope.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-11 primary/blind legal matrices
rootCause: Stellar product-role terminology was promoted into a legal classification.
Registration, licensing, custody, and multi-entity routes were collapsed.
